text: 1. Appearances, etc., may be in person, by recognized agent or by pleader. - Any appearance, application or act in or to any Court, required or authorized by law to be made or done by a party in such Court, may, except where otherwise expressly provided by any law for the time being in force, be made or done by the party in person, or by his recognized agent, or by a pleader [appearing, applying or acting, as the case may be,] [ Substituted by Act 22 of 1926, Section 2, for " duly appointed to act" .] on his behalf: Provide that any such appearance shall, if the Court so directs, be made by the party in person.
